snowflake_spec_version: 2026-07 local sync (curated common-function signatures; refresh from docs.snowflake.com)

signature_legend.signature: call shape with <placeholders>; [] marks optional parts
signature_legend.returns: result type as documented, simplified to the common case
signature_legend.parenless: true when Snowflake also accepts the function without parentheses

functions.name: COUNT
functions.summary: Counts rows (*) or non-NULL values; also usable as a window function with OVER.

functions.name: COUNT_IF
functions.summary: Counts the rows where the boolean condition is TRUE.

functions.name: SUM
functions.summary: Adds the non-NULL input values; returns NULL for an empty or all-NULL group.

functions.name: AVG
functions.summary: Arithmetic mean of the non-NULL input values.

functions.name: MIN
functions.summary: Smallest non-NULL value in the group; NULLs are ignored.

functions.name: MAX
functions.summary: Largest non-NULL value in the group; NULLs are ignored.

functions.name: MEDIAN
functions.summary: Median of the non-NULL values; interpolates between the two middle values.

functions.name: ANY_VALUE
functions.summary: Returns an arbitrary value from the group; cheap way to carry a column through GROUP BY.

functions.name: LISTAGG
functions.summary: Concatenates non-NULL values into one string, optionally ordered and delimited.

functions.name: ARRAY_AGG
functions.summary: Collects the non-NULL input values into an ARRAY, optionally ordered.

functions.name: OBJECT_AGG
functions.summary: Builds one OBJECT per group from key/value pairs; keys must be unique and non-NULL.

functions.name: APPROX_COUNT_DISTINCT
functions.summary: HyperLogLog estimate of the number of distinct values (alias HLL); much cheaper than COUNT(DISTINCT ...).

functions.name: PERCENTILE_CONT
functions.summary: Continuous percentile: interpolates the value at the given fraction of the ordered group.

functions.name: STDDEV
functions.summary: Sample standard deviation (alias STDDEV_SAMP); use STDDEV_POP for the population form.

functions.name: ROW_NUMBER
functions.summary: Unique 1-based row number within each partition in ORDER BY order.

functions.name: RANK
functions.summary: Rank with gaps: ties share a rank and the next rank skips ahead.

functions.name: DENSE_RANK
functions.summary: Rank without gaps: ties share a rank and the next distinct value gets the next rank.

functions.name: NTILE
functions.summary: Splits each partition into <n> ordered buckets and returns the bucket number.

functions.name: LAG
functions.summary: Value from a preceding row in the partition; <default> replaces the out-of-window NULL.

functions.name: LEAD
functions.summary: Value from a following row in the partition; <default> replaces the out-of-window NULL.

functions.name: FIRST_VALUE
functions.signature: FIRST_VALUE( <expr> ) [ IGNORE NULLS ] OVER ( [PARTITION BY ...] ORDER BY ... )
functions.summary: First value of the ordered window frame; IGNORE NULLS skips leading NULLs.

functions.name: LAST_VALUE
functions.signature: LAST_VALUE( <expr> ) [ IGNORE NULLS ] OVER ( [PARTITION BY ...] ORDER BY ... )
functions.summary: Last value of the ordered window frame; mind the default frame end of CURRENT ROW.

functions.name: RATIO_TO_REPORT
functions.summary: Each row's share of the partition total: value / SUM(value) OVER (...).

functions.name: CONCAT
functions.summary: Concatenates strings; equivalent to the || operator, and NULL if any input is NULL.

functions.name: CONCAT_WS
functions.summary: Concatenates with a separator between the inputs; NULL if any input is NULL.

functions.name: SUBSTR
functions.summary: Substring from 1-based <start> (alias SUBSTRING); a negative start counts from the end.

functions.name: SPLIT
functions.summary: Splits a string on the literal separator into an ARRAY of strings.

functions.name: SPLIT_PART
functions.summary: Returns the 1-based Nth piece after splitting; a negative index counts from the end.

functions.name: TRIM
functions.summary: Removes leading and trailing whitespace, or any of the given characters.

functions.name: LPAD
functions.summary: Pads (or truncates) the string on the left to <length> using <pad>, default blanks.

functions.name: RPAD
functions.summary: Pads (or truncates) the string on the right to <length> using <pad>, default blanks.

functions.name: UPPER
functions.summary: Uppercases the string.

functions.name: LOWER
functions.summary: Lowercases the string.

functions.name: INITCAP
functions.summary: Capitalizes the first letter of each word; <delimiters> overrides the word separators.

functions.name: LENGTH
functions.summary: Length in characters for strings (alias LEN); bytes for BINARY.

functions.name: REPLACE
functions.summary: Replaces every occurrence of the literal pattern; omitting <replacement> deletes it.

functions.name: POSITION
functions.summary: 1-based position of the first occurrence, or 0 when not found (alias CHARINDEX with swapped args).

functions.name: STARTSWITH
functions.summary: TRUE when the string starts with the prefix (case-sensitive).

functions.name: ENDSWITH
functions.summary: TRUE when the string ends with the suffix (case-sensitive).

functions.name: CONTAINS
functions.summary: TRUE when the first argument contains the second (case-sensitive).

functions.name: REGEXP_REPLACE
functions.summary: Regex replace; the default empty <replacement> deletes matches, and backslashes must be doubled in string literals.

functions.name: REGEXP_SUBSTR
functions.summary: Returns the matching substring (or capture group) for the Nth regex match, or NULL.

functions.name: COALESCE
functions.summary: First non-NULL argument, evaluated left to right.

functions.name: NULLIF
functions.summary: NULL when both arguments are equal, otherwise the first argument.

functions.name: IFF
functions.summary: Single-branch if/then/else; NULL conditions fall through to <false_result>.

functions.name: IFNULL
functions.summary: Second argument when the first is NULL (alias NVL).

functions.name: NVL
functions.summary: Second argument when the first is NULL (alias of IFNULL).

functions.name: NVL2
functions.summary: Returns the second argument when the first is not NULL, otherwise the third.

functions.name: ZEROIFNULL
functions.summary: 0 when the input is NULL, otherwise the input.

functions.name: DECODE
functions.summary: Compares against each search value (NULL matches NULL) and returns the paired result.

functions.name: GREATEST
functions.summary: Largest argument; NULL if any argument is NULL (see GREATEST_IGNORE_NULLS).

functions.name: LEAST
functions.summary: Smallest argument; NULL if any argument is NULL (see LEAST_IGNORE_NULLS).

functions.name: EQUAL_NULL
functions.summary: NULL-safe equality: TRUE when both are equal or both are NULL.

functions.name: ABS
functions.summary: Absolute value.

functions.name: CEIL
functions.summary: Rounds up (toward positive infinity) to the given scale, default 0 digits.

functions.name: FLOOR
functions.summary: Rounds down (toward negative infinity) to the given scale, default 0 digits.

functions.name: ROUND
functions.summary: Rounds half away from zero by default; HALF_TO_EVEN is available as the rounding mode.

functions.name: TRUNC
functions.summary: Truncates toward zero at the given scale; the two-argument date form works like DATE_TRUNC.

functions.name: MOD
functions.summary: Remainder with the sign of the dividend; also available as the % operator.

functions.name: POWER
functions.summary: Raises the base to the exponent (alias POW).

functions.name: SQRT
functions.summary: Square root; errors on negative input.

functions.name: RANDOM
functions.summary: Pseudo-random 64-bit integer per row; a seed makes the sequence repeatable within a query.

functions.name: UNIFORM
functions.summary: Uniformly distributed value in [min, max]; pass RANDOM() as the generator.

functions.name: CURRENT_DATE
functions.summary: Current date in the session time zone.

functions.name: CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
functions.summary: Current timestamp in the session time zone.

functions.name: SYSDATE
functions.summary: Current timestamp in UTC, independent of the session time zone.

functions.name: CURRENT_USER
functions.summary: Name of the user who is running the session.

functions.name: CURRENT_ROLE
functions.summary: Primary role active in the session, or NULL when only secondary roles are active.

functions.name: CURRENT_WAREHOUSE
functions.summary: Name of the warehouse in use for the session.

functions.name: CURRENT_DATABASE
functions.summary: Database in use for the session, or NULL when none is selected.

functions.name: CURRENT_SCHEMA
functions.summary: Schema in use for the session, or NULL when none is selected.

functions.name: DATEADD
functions.summary: Adds the signed value of the given part (day, month, hour, ...) to a date/time.

functions.name: DATEDIFF
functions.summary: Counts part boundaries crossed between start and end (not elapsed time): end minus start.

functions.name: DATE_TRUNC
functions.summary: Truncates a date/time down to the start of the given part (year, month, day, hour, ...).

functions.name: DATE_PART
functions.summary: Extracts the numeric value of the given part; EXTRACT(<part> FROM <expr>) is equivalent.

functions.name: EXTRACT
functions.summary: SQL-standard spelling of DATE_PART.

functions.name: LAST_DAY
functions.summary: Last day of the month (default) or of the given part, e.g. year or quarter.

functions.name: CONVERT_TIMEZONE
functions.summary: Converts between IANA time zones; the two-argument form treats the input as TIMESTAMP_LTZ.

functions.name: TO_VARCHAR
functions.summary: Converts to text, optionally with a number or date/time format model (alias TO_CHAR).

functions.name: TO_NUMBER
functions.summary: Converts to a fixed-point number (alias TO_DECIMAL); errors on invalid input, see TRY_TO_NUMBER.

functions.name: TO_DATE
functions.summary: Converts strings, timestamps, or epoch numbers to DATE; errors on invalid input, see TRY_TO_DATE.

functions.name: TO_TIMESTAMP
functions.summary: Converts to a timestamp; _NTZ/_LTZ/_TZ variants pick the time zone flavor explicitly.

functions.name: TRY_TO_NUMBER
functions.summary: Like TO_NUMBER but returns NULL instead of erroring on unparsable input.

functions.name: TRY_TO_DATE
functions.summary: Like TO_DATE but returns NULL instead of erroring on unparsable input.

functions.name: TRY_TO_TIMESTAMP
functions.summary: Like TO_TIMESTAMP but returns NULL instead of erroring on unparsable input.

functions.name: TO_JSON
functions.summary: Serializes a VARIANT value to a JSON string; inverse of PARSE_JSON.

functions.name: PARSE_JSON
functions.summary: Parses JSON text into a VARIANT; errors on invalid JSON, see TRY_PARSE_JSON.

functions.name: TRY_PARSE_JSON
functions.summary: Like PARSE_JSON but returns NULL instead of erroring on invalid JSON.

functions.name: FLATTEN
functions.signature: FLATTEN( [INPUT =>] <expr> [, PATH => <path>] [, OUTER => TRUE|FALSE] [, RECURSIVE => TRUE|FALSE] [, MODE => 'OBJECT'|'ARRAY'|'BOTH'] )
functions.returns: rows: SEQ, KEY, PATH, INDEX, VALUE, THIS
functions.summary: Table function that explodes an ARRAY/OBJECT into rows; use with LATERAL or TABLE(...).

functions.name: GENERATOR
functions.summary: Table function producing synthetic rows; pair with SEQ4()/RANDOM() inside TABLE(...).

functions.name: SEQ4
functions.summary: Sequence of 4-byte integers for generated rowsets (also SEQ1/SEQ2/SEQ8); may contain gaps — use ROW_NUMBER for gap-free numbering.

functions.name: GET
functions.summary: Element by object key or 0-based array index; NULL when absent. Bracket/colon syntax compiles to this.

functions.name: GET_PATH
functions.summary: Extracts a nested element by path such as 'a.b[0].c'; the : path operator is shorthand for this.

functions.name: OBJECT_CONSTRUCT
functions.summary: Builds an OBJECT from key/value pairs or all columns (*); pairs with NULL key or value are dropped (see OBJECT_CONSTRUCT_KEEP_NULL).

functions.name: OBJECT_KEYS
functions.summary: Top-level keys of an OBJECT (or object-typed VARIANT) as an array of strings.

functions.name: ARRAY_CONSTRUCT
functions.summary: Builds an ARRAY from the arguments; [a, b, c] literal syntax is equivalent.

functions.name: ARRAY_SIZE
functions.summary: Number of elements in an ARRAY (or array-typed VARIANT); NULL for non-arrays.

functions.name: ARRAY_CONTAINS
functions.summary: TRUE when the array contains the value; note the value comes first.

functions.name: ARRAY_TO_STRING
functions.summary: Joins array elements into one string with the separator; NULL elements become empty strings.

functions.name: TYPEOF
functions.summary: Type of the value stored in a VARIANT, e.g. 'VARCHAR', 'INTEGER', 'OBJECT', 'ARRAY', 'NULL_VALUE'.

functions.name: HASH
functions.summary: Stable (non-cryptographic) 64-bit signed hash of the arguments; do not use for security.

functions.name: MD5
functions.summary: MD5 digest as 32 hex characters.

functions.name: SHA2
functions.summary: SHA-2 digest as hex; digest size 224, 256 (default), 384, or 512.

functions.name: UUID_STRING
functions.summary: Random version-4 UUID, or a deterministic version-5 UUID from a namespace and name.

functions.name: SYSTEM$STREAM_HAS_DATA
functions.summary: TRUE when a stream has change records to consume; the usual guard in a task WHEN clause.

functions.name: AI_COMPLETE
functions.summary: Generates an LLM completion for the prompt; the AISQL successor to SNOWFLAKE.CORTEX.COMPLETE.

functions.name: SNOWFLAKE.CORTEX.COMPLETE
functions.summary: LLM completion with a chosen model; newer workloads should prefer AI_COMPLETE.

functions.name: SNOWFLAKE.CORTEX.SENTIMENT
functions.summary: Sentiment score from -1 (negative) to 1 (positive) for English text.

functions.name: SNOWFLAKE.CORTEX.TRANSLATE
functions.summary: Translates text between supported languages; '' as source language enables auto-detection.
